forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KY5QYYC0V75VBPP5T534ZRK0
+- 01KY57RS5KNHPWKK23SQD0P6VT
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8780'
+title: 'ShellTool: post completion and throttled progress events from detached commands'
+---
+## What
+
+Make the fused `shell` tool an `EventEmittingTool` (per the OperationTool event task): a detached command's supervisor posts out-of-band events so a session host can learn about completion without the model polling. This package depends only on `FoundationModelsOperationTool` ‚Äî never on the Router.
+
+Files:
+- `Sources/ShellTool/ShellContext.swift` ‚Äî adopt the OperationTool opt-in context surface for a connected `OperationEventSink` (optional; absent sink = no events, zero behavior change).
+- `Sources/ShellTool/ShellRunner.swift` (detached path) ‚Äî post events with `tool: "shell"`, `op: "execute command"`, `correlationID: commandID`:
+  - `.completed` ‚Äî exactly once, when a *detached* command finalizes (any of `completed`/`timed_out`/`killed`), carrying command string, final status, exit code, line count, duration ms. A command that finishes within the wait window posts nothing ‚Äî its result was already delivered in-band.
+  - `.progress` ‚Äî throttled at the source to at most one per `progressInterval` (named constant, propose 5s; injectable for tests), carrying line count so far, only while detached.
+- `Sources/ShellTool/ShellTool.swift` ‚Äî `make(...)` plumbing so an embedder (or a host holding `any EventEmittingTool`) can connect a sink; expose the conformance.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] With a fake sink connected: a detached command posts `.completed` exactly once with correct status/exitCode/lineCount; a killed and a timed-out detached command each post `.completed` with their status
+- [ ] A command finishing within the wait window posts no events
+- [ ] `.progress` events respect the throttle interval (assert via injected interval, not wall-clock 5s waits)
+- [ ] No connected sink ‚Üí identical behavior to today (no errors, no retention)
+- [ ] Public API documented per the doc-coverage gate
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] `Tests/ShellToolTests/ShellRunnerTests.swift` (or new `ShellEventsTests.swift`) ‚Äî fake sink actor records events; all criteria above
+- [ ] `swift test` fully green
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

This package depends only on `FoundationModelsOperationTool` ‚Äî never on the Router.
-

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -12,15 +12,18 @@

 Make the fused `shell` tool an `EventEmittingTool` (per the OperationTool event task): a detached command's supervisor posts out-of-band events so a session host can learn about completion without the model polling. This package depends only on `FoundationModelsOperationTool` ‚Äî never on the Router.

+**Subscription contract (pinned):** the embedder never wires events by hand. They drop the fused tool into a session's tool list exactly as today; a host that knows `EventEmittingTool` (e.g. Router's `makeSession(tools:)`) discovers the conformance by casting from `any Tool` and connects its own sink during session setup. This task's job is to make that cast work on the fused `shell` tool and route posted events from the operations' execution paths to whatever sink got connected ‚Äî `connect` is called by hosts, not by users, and a plain `LanguageModelSession` embedder who connects nothing gets today's behavior unchanged.
+
 Files:
 - `Sources/ShellTool/ShellContext.swift` ‚Äî adopt the OperationTool opt-in context surface for a connected `OperationEventSink` (optional; absent sink = no events, zero behavior change).
 - `Sources/ShellTool/ShellRunner.swift` (detached path) ‚Äî post events with `tool: "shell"`, `op: "execute command"`, `correlationID: commandID`:
   - `.completed` ‚Äî exactly once, when a *detached* command finalizes (any of `completed`/`timed_out`/`killed`), carrying command string, final status, exit code, line count, duration ms. A command that finishes within the wait window posts nothing ‚Äî its result was already delivered in-band.
   - `.progress` ‚Äî throttled at the source to at most one per `progressInterval` (named constant, propose 5s; injectable for tests), carrying line count so far, only while detached.
-- `Sources/ShellTool/ShellTool.swift` ‚Äî `make(...)` plumbing so an embedder (or a host holding `any EventEmittingTool`) can connect a sink; expose the conformance.
+- `Sources/ShellTool/ShellTool.swift` ‚Äî ensure the tool `make(...)` returns is discoverable as `any EventEmittingTool` via cast from `any Tool` (the conformance comes from the OperationTool machinery when `ShellContext` opts in; verify and document rather than hand-roll).

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] With a fake sink connected: a detached command posts `.completed` exactly once with correct status/exitCode/lineCount; a killed and a timed-out detached command each post `.completed` with their status
+- [ ] The value returned by `ShellTool.make()` casts to `any EventEmittingTool` from an `any Tool` existential ‚Äî verified by a test performing exactly that cast, no ShellTool-specific knowledge
+- [ ] With a sink connected via that cast: a detached command posts `.completed` exactly once with correct status/exitCode/lineCount; a killed and a timed-out detached command each post `.completed` with their status
 - [ ] A command finishing within the wait window posts no events
 - [ ] `.progress` events respect the throttle interval (assert via injected interval, not wall-clock 5s waits)
 - [ ] No connected sink ‚Üí identical behavior to today (no errors, no retention)
@@ -27,8 +30,8 @@
 - [ ] Public API documented per the doc-coverage gate

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `Tests/ShellToolTests/ShellRunnerTests.swift` (or new `ShellEventsTests.swift`) ‚Äî fake sink actor records events; all criteria above
+- [ ] `Tests/ShellToolTests/ShellRunnerTests.swift` (or new `ShellEventsTests.swift`) ‚Äî cast-and-connect from `any Tool`; fake sink actor records events; all criteria above
 - [ ] `swift test` fully green

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass. #long-running
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

 - [ ] `swift test` fully green

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -11,28 +11,29 @@

 Make the fused `shell` tool an `EventEmittingTool`: a detached command's supervisor posts out-of-band events so a session host can learn about completion without the model polling. This package depends only on `FoundationModelsOperationTool` ‚Äî never on the Router.

-**Cross-repo prerequisite (prose, not a board dependency):** requires the OperationTool events feature (`OperationEvent` / `OperationEventSink` / `EventEmittingTool`, task `fbyt04c` on the board in `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/FoundationModelsOperationTool`, `long-running` tag) to have landed and this package's `FoundationModelsOperationTool` dependency to resolve a version containing it. Verify before starting; if absent, stop and report rather than hand-rolling substitute protocols.
+**Cross-repo prerequisite (prose, not a board dependency):** requires the OperationTool **per-session instancing** rework (`EventEmittingTool.connecting(_:) -> any Tool` replacing the removed `connect(_:)` mutation and `OperationEventSinkHolder` ‚Äî task on the board in `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/FoundationModelsOperationTool`, `long-running` tag) to have landed and this package's dependency to resolve a version containing it. Verify before starting; if absent, stop and report rather than hand-rolling substitute protocols.

-**Subscription contract (pinned):** the embedder never wires events by hand. They drop the fused tool into a session's tool list exactly as today; a host that knows `EventEmittingTool` (e.g. Router's `makeSession(tools:)`) discovers the conformance by casting from `any Tool` and connects its own sink during session setup. This task's job is to make that cast work on the fused `shell` tool and route posted events from the operations' execution paths to whatever sink got connected ‚Äî `connect` is called by hosts, not by users, and a plain `LanguageModelSession` embedder who connects nothing gets today's behavior unchanged.
+**Subscription contract (pinned):** the embedder never wires events by hand. A host that knows `EventEmittingTool` (e.g. Router) maps its `[any Tool]` list through `connecting(sessionSink)` during session setup and hands the *instanced copies* to the model; a plain `LanguageModelSession` embedder may call `shell.connecting(mySink)` directly. Instances returned by `connecting` share the underlying `ShellState`/supervisor (one command history, one `.shell` store) ‚Äî only the event route differs per copy.

 Files:
-- `Sources/ShellTool/ShellContext.swift` ‚Äî adopt the OperationTool opt-in context surface for a connected `OperationEventSink` (optional; absent sink = no events, zero behavior change).
-- `Sources/ShellTool/ShellRunner.swift` (detached path) ‚Äî post events with `tool: "shell"`, `op: "execute command"`, `correlationID: commandID`:
+- `Sources/ShellTool/ShellContext.swift` ‚Äî conform to the OperationTool `EventEmittingContext` value surface: `let operationEventSink: (any OperationEventSink)?` (default `nil` = no events, zero behavior change) plus the `connecting(_:) -> Self` copy that replaces the sink while sharing `state`/`runner`/`policy` by reference/value as appropriate.
+- `Sources/ShellTool/ShellRunner.swift` (detached path) ‚Äî **capture the context's sink once, at operation start**, into the detached supervisor (the capture-at-start rule: ownership stays with the session whose turn started the command, regardless of later re-instancing or forks). Post events with `tool: "shell"`, `op: "execute command"`, `correlationID: commandID`:
   - `.completed` ‚Äî exactly once, when a *detached* command finalizes (any of `completed`/`timed_out`/`killed`), carrying command string, final status, exit code, line count, duration ms. A command that finishes within the wait window posts nothing ‚Äî its result was already delivered in-band.
   - `.progress` ‚Äî throttled at the source to at most one per `progressInterval` (named constant, propose 5s; injectable for tests), carrying line count so far, only while detached.
-- `Sources/ShellTool/ShellTool.swift` ‚Äî ensure the tool `make(...)` returns is discoverable as `any EventEmittingTool` via cast from `any Tool` (the conformance comes from the OperationTool machinery when `ShellContext` opts in; verify and document rather than hand-roll).
+- `Sources/ShellTool/ShellTool.swift` ‚Äî verify the tool `make(...)` returns is discoverable as `any EventEmittingTool` via cast from `any Tool` and that `connecting` yields a working sink-bound copy (conformance comes from the OperationTool machinery when `ShellContext` opts in; verify and document rather than hand-roll).

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] The value returned by `ShellTool.make()` casts to `any EventEmittingTool` from an `any Tool` existential ‚Äî verified by a test performing exactly that cast, no ShellTool-specific knowledge
-- [ ] With a sink connected via that cast: a detached command posts `.completed` exactly once with correct status/exitCode/lineCount; a killed and a timed-out detached command each post `.completed` with their status
-- [ ] A command finishing within the wait window posts no events
+- [ ] The value returned by `ShellTool.make()` casts to `any EventEmittingTool` from an `any Tool` existential, and `connecting(fakeSink)` returns a usable tool copy ‚Äî verified by a test performing exactly that cast-and-connecting, no ShellTool-specific knowledge
+- [ ] Two `connecting` copies over one `make()` result share command history (a command run via copy A is visible via `list processes` through copy B) while posting events to their own sinks only
+- [ ] With a sink-bound copy: a detached command posts `.completed` exactly once with correct status/exitCode/lineCount; a killed and a timed-out detached command each post `.completed` with their status
+- [ ] A command finishing within the wait window posts no events; the un-instanced original posts nothing anywhere
 - [ ] `.progress` events respect the throttle interval (assert via injected interval, not wall-clock 5s waits)
-- [ ] No connected sink ‚Üí identical behavior to today (no errors, no retention)
+- [ ] Sink captured at operation start: re-instancing (or discarding) the copy after a command detaches does not change where that command's events go
 - [ ] Public API documented per the doc-coverage gate

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `Tests/ShellToolTests/ShellRunnerTests.swift` (or new `ShellEventsTests.swift`) ‚Äî cast-and-connect from `any Tool`; fake sink actor records events; all criteria above
+- [ ] `Tests/ShellToolTests/ShellRunnerTests.swift` (or new `ShellEventsTests.swift`) ‚Äî cast-and-connecting from `any Tool`; shared-state/independent-route test; fake sink actor records events; all criteria above
 - [ ] `swift test` fully green

 ## Workflow
-- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass. #long-running
\ No newline at end of file
+- Use `/tdd` ‚Äî write failing tests first, then implement to make them pass.
\ No newline at end of file

+- `Sources/ShellTool/ShellTool.swift` ‚Äî ensure the tool `make(...)` returns is discoverable as `any EventEmittingTool` via cast from `any Tool` (the conformance comes from the OperationTool machinery when `ShellContext` opts in; verify and document rather than hand-roll).

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] The value returned by `ShellTool.make()` casts to `any EventEmittingTool` from an `any Tool` existential, and `connecting(fakeSink)` returns a usable tool copy ‚Äî verified by a test performing exactly that cast-and-connecting, no ShellTool-specific knowledge
-- [ ] Two `connecting` copies over one `make()` result share command history (a command run via copy A is visible via `list processes` through copy B) while posting events to their own sinks only

-- [ ] A command finishing within the wait window posts no events; the un-instanced original posts nothing anywhere

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -11,29 +11,31 @@

 Make the fused `shell` tool an `EventEmittingTool`: a detached command's supervisor posts out-of-band events so a session host can learn about completion without the model polling. This package depends only on `FoundationModelsOperationTool` ‚Äî never on the Router.

-**Cross-repo prerequisite (prose, not a board dependency):** requires the OperationTool **per-session instancing** rework (`EventEmittingTool.connecting(_:) -> any Tool` replacing the removed `connect(_:)` mutation and `OperationEventSinkHolder` ‚Äî task on the board in `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/FoundationModelsOperationTool`, `long-running` tag) to have landed and this package's dependency to resolve a version containing it. Verify before starting; if absent, stop and report rather than hand-rolling substitute protocols.
+**Cross-repo prerequisite (prose, not a board dependency):** requires the OperationTool **pure tool-capabilities** rework (`EventEmittingTool.connecting(_:) -> any Tool` + `ForkableTool.forked()` with blanket copy default, replacing the removed `connect(_:)` mutation and `OperationEventSinkHolder` ‚Äî task `f7jjm6x` on the board in `/Users/wballard/github/swissarmyhammer/FoundationModelsOperationTool`, `long-running` tag) to have landed and this package's dependency to resolve a version containing it. Verify before starting; if absent, stop and report rather than hand-rolling substitute protocols.

 **Subscription contract (pinned):** the embedder never wires events by hand. A host that knows `EventEmittingTool` (e.g. Router) maps its `[any Tool]` list through `connecting(sessionSink)` during session setup and hands the *instanced copies* to the model; a plain `LanguageModelSession` embedder may call `shell.connecting(mySink)` directly. Instances returned by `connecting` share the underlying `ShellState`/supervisor (one command history, one `.shell` store) ‚Äî only the event route differs per copy.

+**Fork stance (pinned for v1):** the shell relies on the **blanket `forked()` default** (a struct copy sharing the engine by reference) ‚Äî `ShellContext` does NOT implement `ForkableContext` in v1. One shared machine: parent and fork share command history; each session's events route through its own `connecting` copy. Branched history is a possible later tool-internal upgrade; do not build it here.
+
 Files:
-- `Sources/ShellTool/ShellContext.swift` ‚Äî conform to the OperationTool `EventEmittingContext` value surface: `let operationEventSink: (any OperationEventSink)?` (default `nil` = no events, zero behavior change) plus the `connecting(_:) -> Self` copy that replaces the sink while sharing `state`/`runner`/`policy` by reference/value as appropriate.
+- `Sources/ShellTool/ShellContext.swift` ‚Äî conform to the OperationTool `EventEmittingContext` value surface: `let operationEventSink: (any OperationEventSink)?` (default `nil` = no events, zero behavior change) plus the `connecting(_:) -> Self` copy that replaces the sink while sharing `state`/`runner`/`policy`.
 - `Sources/ShellTool/ShellRunner.swift` (detached path) ‚Äî **capture the context's sink once, at operation start**, into the detached supervisor (the capture-at-start rule: ownership stays with the session whose turn started the command, regardless of later re-instancing or forks). Post events with `tool: "shell"`, `op: "execute command"`, `correlationID: commandID`:
   - `.completed` ‚Äî exactly once, when a *detached* command finalizes (any of `completed`/`timed_out`/`killed`), carrying command string, final status, exit code, line count, duration ms. A command that finishes within the wait window posts nothing ‚Äî its result was already delivered in-band.
   - `.progress` ‚Äî throttled at the source to at most one per `progressInterval` (named constant, propose 5s; injectable for tests), carrying line count so far, only while detached.
-- `Sources/ShellTool/ShellTool.swift` ‚Äî verify the tool `make(...)` returns is discoverable as `any EventEmittingTool` via cast from `any Tool` and that `connecting` yields a working sink-bound copy (conformance comes from the OperationTool machinery when `ShellContext` opts in; verify and document rather than hand-roll).
+- `Sources/ShellTool/ShellTool.swift` ‚Äî verify the tool `make(...)` returns is discoverable as `any EventEmittingTool` AND `any ForkableTool` via cast from `any Tool`, that `connecting` yields a working sink-bound copy, and that the blanket `forked()` copy shares the engine (conformances come from the OperationTool machinery; verify and document rather than hand-roll).

 ## Acceptance Criteria
-- [ ] The value returned by `ShellTool.make()` casts to `any EventEmittingTool` from an `any Tool` existential, and `connecting(fakeSink)` returns a usable tool copy ‚Äî verified by a test performing exactly that cast-and-connecting, no ShellTool-specific knowledge
-- [ ] Two `connecting` copies over one `make()` result share command history (a command run via copy A is visible via `list processes` through copy B) while posting events to their own sinks only
+- [ ] The value returned by `ShellTool.make()` casts to both `any EventEmittingTool` and `any ForkableTool` from an `any Tool` existential; `connecting(fakeSink)` returns a usable tool copy ‚Äî verified by tests performing exactly those casts, no ShellTool-specific knowledge
+- [ ] Two `connecting` copies over one `make()` result share command history (a command run via copy A is visible via `list processes` through copy B) while posting events to their own sinks only; a `forked()` copy likewise shares history (one shared machine)
 - [ ] With a sink-bound copy: a detached command posts `.completed` exactly once with correct status/exitCode/lineCount; a killed and a timed-out detached command each post `.completed` with their status
 - [ ] A command finishing within the wait window posts no events; the un-instanced original posts nothing anywhere
 - [ ] `.progress` events respect the throttle interval (assert via injected interval, not wall-clock 5s waits)
 - [ ] Sink captured at operation start: re-instancing (or discarding) the copy after a command detaches does not change where that command's events go
-- [ ] Public API documented per the doc-coverage gate
+- [ ] Public API documented per the doc-coverage gate, including the v1 fork stance

 ## Tests
-- [ ] `Tests/ShellToolTests/ShellRunnerTests.swift` (or new `ShellEventsTests.swift`) ‚Äî cast-and-connecting from `any Tool`; shared-state/independent-route test; fake sink actor records events; all criteria above
+- [ ] `Tests/ShellToolTests/ShellRunnerTests.swift` (or new `ShellEventsTests.swift`) ‚Äî cast-and-connecting/forked from `any Tool`; shared-state/independent-route tests; fake sink actor records events; all criteria above
 - [ ] `swift test` fully green

\ No newline at end of file
